spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (navigator., document.querySelector) with runtime script (~6271 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 11 buttons, 2 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (admin, allowlist, chrome, console, domains, enterprise, json, output, policies, policy, requests, select, type, which, w).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Connection Allowlists is a feature designed to provide explicit control over external endpoints by restricting connections initiated via the Fetch API or other web platform APIs from a document or worker.  The proposed implementation involv") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://wicg.github.io/connection-allowlists to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~6271 chars of script across 11 button(s) and 2 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 1 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
